title: Multiplayer Drawing Room with Voice Messaging

requirements.text: Add a backend room system: users can create a named room (generating a unique room code) or join an existing room by entering a room code. The backend must store room state including a list of active participants and all drawing strokes (as serialized canvas data or stroke arrays) so late joiners can see the current canvas state.

quotes: people can enter the room and all can draw in it

acceptanceCriteria: A createRoom function exists and returns a unique room code
A joinRoom function accepts a room code and registers a participant
The backend stores canvas strokes/state per room
A new joiner can retrieve the existing canvas state for a room

requirements.text: Implement real-time collaborative drawing: all participants in a room share a single canvas. When one user draws a stroke, it is persisted to the backend and all other participants polling the room receive the new strokes and see them rendered on their canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a function to submit a new stroke to a room
Backend exposes a function to poll/fetch new strokes since a given timestamp or sequence number
Frontend polls for new strokes at a short interval (e.g., 1–2 seconds) and renders them on the shared canvas
Multiple users drawing simultaneously results in all strokes appearing for all participants

requirements.text: Implement in-room voice messaging: users can record a short voice message, which is encoded as base64 audio (WebM/Opus or similar) and stored in the backend against the room. All participants in the room can see and play back received voice messages in a message list panel.

quotes: can also send voice messages to each other

acceptanceCriteria: A record button is visible in the room UI; pressing and holding records audio, releasing stops recording
The recorded audio blob is base64-encoded and sent to the backend via a sendVoiceMessage function
The backend stores voice messages per room with a sender identifier and timestamp
All room participants polling for messages receive new voice messages and can play them back via an audio element
Voice messages appear in a chronological list alongside a playback control

requirements.text: Build a Room UI: replace the existing sender/recipient pairing flow with a unified room-based lobby. The home page should offer 'Create Room' and 'Join Room' options. After entering/creating a room, users land on a room page that shows: the shared drawing canvas (with color and stroke controls), a voice message panel with record button and message history, a participant count or list, and the room code for sharing.

quotes: send voice messages to each other

acceptanceCriteria: Home page shows 'Create Room' and 'Join Room' options
Join Room accepts a room code input
Room page displays a full collaborative canvas matching the existing DrawingCanvas component style
Room page shows a voice message section with a record button and scrollable list of received voice messages
Room code is displayed on the room page so users can share it
Participant count is visible
Visual style is consistent with the existing pastel/doodle aesthetic

requirements.text: Update the TanStack Router routes to include a /room/:code route for the shared room page, and update App.tsx accordingly. Preserve existing / route for the updated home/lobby page.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /room/:code renders the room page with the correct room code
Navigating to / shows the lobby with Create/Join options
No broken routes from previous sender/recipient flow (or they are cleanly removed if replaced)

constraints: All backend logic must remain in the single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Voice messages must be stored as base64-encoded strings in the Motoko backend (no external storage)
Use polling (not WebSockets) for real-time updates, consistent with existing useQueries.ts patterns
Maintain the existing pastel/doodle visual theme from index.css and tailwind.config.js
nonGoals: User authentication or Internet Identity login for room access
Persistent rooms across canister upgrades (in-memory state is acceptable)
Video calling or live audio streaming
